good: Real autonomous payment agent on testnet2. Shows actual fired-rule counts with timestamps (not just claims), a real wallet/nametag/chainPubkey, open-source with README, and is refreshingly transparent that it never exposes secrets to the page. Rules (notify/auto-fee/split/conditional/balance-watch) genuinely fire without human approval.
missing: Nothing major. Could add a public activity feed screenshot/video in the README for reviewers who don't want to send a test payment themselves.

good: Textbook agent-to-agent negotiate-and-settle: BuyerBot/SellerBot discover each other, negotiate via Nostr DMs, escrow-settle on testnet2. Explicitly labelled with its tracks and states 'AstridOS Ready' (eligible for the +500 XP bonus). Clean architecture diagram, matches the official example idea almost exactly.
missing: Confirm the 'Start Live Agent Demo' actually executes a real negotiation end-to-end rather than a scripted animation before awarding Gold.

good: Exceptional craft and writing. Correctly explains Unicity's actual bearer-token/proof-of-uniqueness model in plain language, ships real Sphere wallet connect, signed conditional 'pacts' with templates (bounty, whitelist, escrow, etc.), and explicitly designs for agent use. No server, no custody of funds.
missing: Only one builder (@0XM0RA) - would be good to verify a real end-to-end pact settlement, but conceptually and technically this is one of the strongest submissions in the queue.

good: Wallet-based deliberation forum with genuine UCT tipping, verification tiers paid in UCT, and a v2.1 peer-to-peer hiring/escrow feature (list a job, negotiate by DM, escrow, release, rate). Loads as a Sphere Agent iframe with silent auto-connect. Very polished copy and UX.
missing: Escrow is described as custodial (same treasury wallet holds funds) rather than trustless - fine to disclose, just worth flagging to devs so they describe it accurately if featured.

good: Genuinely novel: an AI social-deduction (mafia-style) game where LLM agents play each other, commit-reveal fairness, prize pool settled on-chain, testnet2. Strong originality relative to the 5+ 2048/coinflip clones in the queue.
missing: Confirm the prize pool payout is a real on-chain settlement and not just a UI animation at round end.

good: Real prediction-market pattern matching the official 'lottery/prediction app' example idea: stake UCT on whether a claim becomes true, community votes, 'connect wallet to vote', built-in leaderboard/WishScore.
missing: Text extraction only showed one active wish - worth checking there's enough real content/volume for reviewers to interact with meaningfully.

good: Deep, correct SDK use: sphere.market bulletin board, Nostr DM negotiation, atomic swap escrow, all explicitly named. Has a 'Launch Autonomous Agents' button to run a live two-agent demo in-browser.
missing: Agents were idle (not started) at check time - re-run the live demo to confirm it actually completes a swap rather than stalling.

good: Genuine network analytics/visualization dashboard. Shows real SDK version (v0.11.3), correct testnet2 gateway id, live latency reading (409ms), and a live SDK event feed - the kind of specific detail a fake wouldn't bother including.
missing: Most panels read 'Not connected' / '—' until a wallet is connected - fine, but worth connecting a test wallet to confirm the live data actually populates.

good: Agentic task marketplace: agents bid on and complete tasks autonomously, backed by live Supabase data, explicit Sphere SDK v0.10.3, uses Groq llama-3.3-70b for agent reasoning.
missing: Board was empty (0 open tasks) at check time - needs seeded/demo tasks so reviewers and users see it in action rather than an empty state.

good: P2P marketplace for AI services explicitly built on atomic swaps and Nostr, matching real SDK primitives. Clean onboarding flow (claim a nametag identity).
missing: Only saw the onboarding screen - would need to claim an identity to verify the actual marketplace/listing flow works end-to-end.

good: Real storefront pattern: live catalog (Steam/Google Play/Amazon/Netflix vouchers priced in UCT), real checkout flow with coinId/testnet2 network shown, 'Pay with Sphere' intent. Commendably honest in-app about its own limitations.
missing: Self-discloses: 'Demo mode - after the Sphere send intent resolves, UniVoucher issues a local demo voucher code. Production must verify payment and fulfill from a server-side supplier.' Fulfillment is not yet real - cap at Silver until that's wired up.

good: Polished provably-fair casino concept, explicit testnet2 + Sphere SDK branding, 'no human in the loop, the agent pays you' autonomy claim, real wallet connect gate.
missing: Verify the jackpot/payout mechanic actually settles on-chain rather than just being a visual multiplier reel.

good: Matches the official 'autonomous treasury' example idea closely: human sets policy limits once (poll interval, target balance, auto-approve cap, daily payout cap), agent operates within bounds. Has a second 'Bob' testbed identity to demonstrate real transfers.
missing: Logs were empty and agent not started at check time - start the agent and Bob to confirm a real autonomous transfer fires.

good: Interesting 'threshold flash-buy swarm' concept - group-buy pools that execute, issue tokens, and refund automatically based on thresholds/deadlines with no human intervention once created.
missing: Active swarms list was stuck on 'Connecting to Agent API...' - backend doesn't appear fully wired up yet.

good: Good concept coverage: treasury manager, intent market, agent directory/reputation, real 'Connect Wallet' CTA, explicit Testnet v2 branding.
missing: Displayed stats (1,204 active agents, 842 open intents, 4.2M UCT in escrow) look implausibly large for an ~88-submission testnet and are likely hardcoded/mocked rather than live - verify before trusting any displayed numbers.

good: Useful dev-support utility: an SDK Q&A chatbot with a real connected wallet (DIRECT:// address shown). Helpful category for onboarding new builders.
missing: Limited to Q&A about the SDK itself rather than doing anything with it economically - low depth of SDK use beyond the wallet connection shown.

good: Creative premise: a 'Game Master' agent vs 'Player/Solver' agent simulation with a live system log feed, Vault Guardian / Solver AI roles.
missing: Only saw an idle 'Awaiting command' state - needs to actually run the simulation to confirm real agent-vs-agent logic (vs. scripted log lines).

good: Basic but functional-looking wallet-connect + clicker game + quest list + leaderboard scaffold.
missing: Hosted on a raw reverse-IP domain (sslip.io) rather than a proper domain - looks unfinished/unpolished; craft is generic template-level.

good: Real agent-to-agent vending pattern matching the official example idea: a live JSON status endpoint shows a real nametag (@eric-autovend), real wallet address, live=true, and DM-based purchase flow (menu / buy <id> / send UCT).
missing: No web UI at all - it's a bare JSON endpoint, so a typical reviewer can't 'see' it working without DMing it from their own Sphere wallet. Fine for an agent-first submission, but craft/presentation score should reflect the missing UI.

good: Multi-game casino hub (Coin Flip, Mines, Dig for Treasure), explicitly built on Sphere SDK + ConnectClient, claims provably-fair + on-chain payout for each game.
missing: Landing page only - would need to actually play a round to confirm payouts are real on-chain settlements rather than client-side random numbers.

good: Round-based lottery/game with real round numbering (Round #4) suggesting genuine recurring rounds rather than a static demo.
missing: Text extraction only caught a 'round settled with no entries' state - no visible wallet-connect UI in the captured text; needs a closer look to confirm SDK depth.

good: Live backend agent process confirmed running ('CoinFlip House Agent is running...').
missing: No visible frontend/UI reachable at the submitted URL - can't assess wallet interaction or craft without more of the app surfaced.

good: Real autonomy pattern described: 'Run autopilot to match an online agent' with policy-based autoselection of a matched agent/payee from a seeded directory.
missing: Page is very sparse (canvas/JS heavy, minimal text extracted) - needs a fuller screenshot/click-through pass to assess craft and depth properly.

good: Best-executed of the several 2048 clones in the queue - has a real economic loop (deposit UCT for move credits, 50% of weekly deposits feeds a top-5 prize pool) rather than just a wallet-gated game.
missing: It's still a 2048 clone at its core - low originality; if featuring a 2048-style game at all, this one edges out the others on depth, but consider whether the category is oversaturated (5+ clones submitted).

good: Simple, functional wallet-gated Snake clone with a stated token-earning mechanic (SNAKE tokens).
missing: Very basic craft/originality - a standard browser game template with a wallet-connect and token bolted on.

good: Functional 2048 clone with wallet connect and daily/all-time leaderboards.
missing: One of at least 5 2048 clones submitted (Brain Game-2048, Sphere 2048, Sphere 2048 Arena, plus 2 dead ones) - low originality relative to Sphere 2048's deposit/prize-pool loop; likely redundant if another 2048 clone is already featured.

good: Extremely ambitious, well-designed multi-agent architecture: 4 autonomous agent processes (Verification/Puzzle/Lore/Treasury), guild system, Sphere wallet handshake, Nostr DM relays, a genuine v1-v4 roadmap including AstridOS WASM capsules.
missing: All 4 agent processes show 'msgs 0' despite being labelled LIVE - the elaborate UI may currently be front-end-only with no real backend traffic yet. Verify actual Nostr message activity before trusting the 'autonomous' claim.

good: Ambitious concept: AI dispute arbitrator monitoring Nostr group chats, Ed25519 signature verification, automated escrow settlement via Sphere SDK.
missing: Internal inconsistency: counters show 'Disputes Handled: 0 / Resolved: 0' while the activity feed simultaneously claims 'Dispute #127 resolved 2 minutes ago' - the live feed looks fabricated/hardcoded rather than reflecting the real counters. Also flagged by another reviewer (1 downvote) and API Health shows 'Degraded.' Needs real verification before any XP is awarded.

good: Polished concept and UI: on-chain reputation/Sybil-detection oracle for Sphere Nametags, paywalled via P2P UCT transfer, AstridOS sandbox bonus logic described.
missing: Score outputs looked inconsistent across different nametags during testing, suggesting the result may be randomized or not fully computed from real on-chain data. Re-verify with multiple nametags before trusting any score it returns.

good: Honest about its own state rather than faking functionality.
missing: Self-admittedly a 'Static reviewer preview' only - 'Live SDK mode is available from the repository run instructions,' meaning the public URL is not actually a live, working app. Fails the spirit of the 'live at a publicly viewable location' eligibility rule as submitted.
Generic template-level game with a 'Connect Wallet' button bolted on - one of 5+ near-identical 2048 submissions in the queue; low originality, minimal SDK depth beyond wallet connect.
Explicitly self-labelled 'Demo App for Unicity Sphere Developer Portal' - a bare click-counter with no wallet connection or SDK use visible at all.

This is the Sphere Connect protocol's own example/demo template (Persian-language 'Sphere Connect Demo', explicitly says 'this page is a sample dApp... ready to register as a project') deployed unmodified. Matches the rules' explicit disqualifier: 'Cosmetic forks or re-skins of the example repositories.'
Explicitly self-labelled 'This is a demo application created for Sphere Developer Portal. Status: Running' - a test scaffold, not a real submission.

Loads with raw unfilled template placeholder text ('hmc_feature_copy_title1' etc.) - looks like an unconfigured white-label Web3 gambling template (sharker.com) rather than a custom Sphere-built app. No evidence of real SDK integration.

No Sphere/Unicity integration of any kind - it's a Spotify-powered DJ song-request app that accepts tips via Revolut, Wise, and USDT (TRC-20/Tron), not the Sphere SDK or UCT at all. Appears to be an unrelated side project submitted to the wrong competition.

'Nexa Premium' - a paid reseller of X (Twitter) Premium subscriptions for $4-$25, contact via email/Discord. No Sphere SDK or wallet integration visible anywhere. Unrelated commercial service, and resold social-media subscription schemes are a known scam-adjacent category - recommend rejecting and not engaging further (no payment was made, no login attempted).

Loads a generic Russian-language account registration form asking for email, phone number, and password - no wallet connect, no visible relation to Unicity/Sphere at all. Already rejected once previously and carries a downvote from another org reviewer. Do not enter any information into this form.

Presents itself as an 'Admin Control Center' ('Much access. Very Google login. Wow.') and prompts to log in with a Google account. No Sphere/wallet integration visible; the meme tone plus a Google-login prompt on an unrelated domain is a phishing-flavored pattern - recommend reject and do not log in with any account.

Uses Capcom's actual 'Captain Commando' game title/branding ('CAPCOM · 1991 · UNICITY EDITION') with a Sphere wallet connect bolted on. This is very likely unauthorized use of third-party copyrighted IP - recommend rejecting outright regardless of technical merit, to avoid any IP exposure if featured publicly.

Submitted app_url is pos.toasttab.com - the real production website of Toast, Inc. (a US restaurant point-of-sale company), completely unrelated to Unicity. Not opened, per standard practice of not visiting links to unrelated third-party commercial domains. Reject outright as an unrelated, spam-like submission.
